LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-18-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DISCOVER BANK'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER IS OFF CALENDAR. THE SHERIFF INFORMED THE COURT THAT THE CREDITOR NEVER SUBMITTED ITS OPPOSITION TO THE CLAIM OF EXEMPTION WITH THE SHERIFF'S DEPARTMENT. THE FUNDS SHALL BE RELEASED TO THE DEBTOR. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Jan-18-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. JONATHAN VALKNIN, Esq. "specially appearing" for Suttell & Hammer A.P.C. (Tel: 888-788-8355) for Plaintiff/Judgment Creditor Discover Bank (via Zoom videoconference;
Defendant/Judgment Debtor DANIEL GREENBERG (Tel: 415-608-4441) in pro per.

PLAINTIFF DISCOVER BANK'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

The Court notes it did not receive a proper notice of contest from any party.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar. The sheriff informed the court that the creditor never submitted its opposition to the claim of exemption with the sheriff's department. The funds shall be released to the debtor.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
